methods.description: Field trips were conducted to the Qa'a Jahran-Dhamār area to collect marl samples. Grey to dark grey marl layers were traced laterally through exposed sections and samples were collected from shallow wells dug by villagers. Sampling was done at intervals, focusing on darker layers, and detailed location and deposit descriptions were recorded.

steps.description: Two field trips were made to the Qa'a Jahran-Dhamār area during November 2013 and March 2014 to collect the material used in the current study.
steps.searchable_summary: conduct field trips to specified geological area for sample collection

steps.description: Grey to dark grey marl layers were traced laterally through eight exposed sections in the northern part of the basin of Qa'a Jahran.
steps.searchable_summary: trace geological layers laterally in exposed sections

steps.description: Forty-three marl samples of variable thickness were collected from those eight sections from eight shallow wells dug by villagers (Figs.S1 and S2 in the Supplement).
steps.searchable_summary: collect marl samples from identified sections and wells

steps.description: They were mostly taken at intervals of 10-20 cm focusing on the darker layers.
steps.searchable_summary: collect samples at specified intervals focusing on specific layer types
outputs: Specific collected samples based on strategy

steps.description: The sampling locations along with geographical coordinates and the deposit descriptions are given in Table 1.
steps.searchable_summary: record metadata for collected samples including location, coordinates, and description
outputs: Table/record of sample metadata (location, coordinates, description)

methods.description: Standard micropalaeontological techniques were applied to the collected marl samples to separate mud and clay from shells, preparing the residue for ostracod extraction. This involved chemical treatment, boiling, washing, and sieving.

steps.description: A unit weight of 250 g (ca. 5 cm thick) of fresh unweathered sediment sample was always taken.
steps.searchable_summary: take a specific unit weight of sediment sample for processing

steps.description: Samples were covered in a pan with 29 % hydrogen peroxide to separate the mud and clay from the shells.
steps.searchable_summary: treat sediment samples with hydrogen peroxide for disaggregation

steps.description: After allowing a sample to soak for about 12 h, water was added and boiled.
steps.searchable_summary: soak samples and then boil with added water

steps.description: The samples were washed with running tap water over a 200 mesh sieve (opening of 0.074 mm) to remove mud-size sediment.
steps.searchable_summary: wash and sieve samples to remove fine sediment

steps.description: About 10 to 20 g of washed residue of sandy sediment, shells and rock fragments were extracted from each sample.
steps.searchable_summary: extract a specific amount of washed residue for further analysis

methods.description: Ostracods were collected from the prepared sediment residue using a stereomicroscope and brush, placed onto micro-slides, and identified.

steps.description: The ostracods were collected by using a 00 brush under a stereomicroscope, placed into micro-slides and identified.
steps.searchable_summary: collect ostracods from residue using microscopy and brush

steps.description: ...placed into micro-slides...
steps.searchable_summary: place collected ostracods onto micro-slides

steps.description: ...and identified.
steps.searchable_summary: identify ostracods to species level

steps.description: No ostracods with complete soft parts were found.
steps.searchable_summary: note the condition of collected specimens (absence of soft parts)

methods.description: Representatives of the identified ostracods were sent to various institutions for Scanning Electron Microscope (SEM) imaging. Specimens of all species were deposited in a university collection, and measurements were recorded.

steps.description: Representatives of the recorded ostracods were sent to Hamburg, Jena and Rome.
steps.searchable_summary: send representative ostracod specimens to external laboratories

steps.description: Scanning electron microscope (SEM) photos were taken at the Zoological Institute and Museum at Hamburg University, the Institute of Zoology at Jena University and the Istituto di Geologia Ambientale e Geoingegneria Roma.
steps.searchable_summary: acquire scanning electron microscope (SEM) images of ostracod specimens

steps.description: Specimens of all ostracod species were deposited in the Department of Earth and Environmental Sciences, Faculty of Science, Sanaa University.
steps.searchable_summary: deposit ostracod specimens in a scientific collection

steps.description: All measurements are given in millimetres.
steps.searchable_summary: record measurements of ostracod specimens

methods.description: Statistical analysis was performed using the program package PAST to support the interpretation of the palaeoenvironments based on the ostracod data.

steps.description: Statistical analysis using the program package PAST (Hammer et al., 2001) was done to support the interpretation of the palaeoenvironments.
steps.searchable_summary: perform statistical analysis on ostracod data using PAST software

steps.description: ...to support the interpretation of the palaeoenvironments.
steps.searchable_summary: interpret palaeoenvironments based on statistical analysis results
